item.title: Loyal Equipments Ltd Falls 2.46%: Technical Upgrade and Valuation Shift Shape Volatile Week
item.description: Loyal Equipments Ltd experienced a turbulent week on the BSE, closing at Rs.180.55 on 6 March 2026, down 2.46% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.185.10. Despite this decline, the stock outperformed the broader Sensex, which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by a significant upgrade in the company’s technical and valuation outlook, a sharp intraday rally, and notable volatility in price and volume, reflecting a complex interplay of cautious optimism and persistent financial challenges. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Ircon International Ltd Gains 2.37%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: Ircon International Ltd’s stock showed a mixed week from 2 to 6 March 2026, closing with a modest gain of 2.37% to Rs.146.95, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp intraday volatility, new 52-week lows early in the week, followed by a strong rebound driven by heavy institutional buying and volume surges on the final trading day. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: W S Industries Falls 1.40%: 2 Key Technical Signals Driving the Decline
item.description: W S Industries (India) Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.69.70 on 6 March 2026, down 1.40% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.70.69. This decline came amid a broader market sell-off, with the Sensex falling 3.00% over the same period. Despite the stock’s relative resilience compared to the benchmark, technical developments including the formation of a Death Cross and a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating intensified bearish sentiment, signalling potential further weakness ahead. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: eClerx Services Ltd Declines 4.80%: Technical Shifts and Market Volatility Shape the Week
item.description: eClerx Services Ltd experienced a turbulent week marked by significant technical momentum shifts and a 4.80% decline in its stock price, closing at Rs.3,021.35 on 6 March 2026. Despite outperforming the Sensex's 3.00% fall over the same period, the stock faced mixed signals from technical indicators and a downgrade to a Hold rating, reflecting cautious investor sentiment amid broader market volatility. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Seasons Textiles Gains 9.44%: Key Valuation Shift Amidst Weak Fundamentals
item.description: Seasons Textiles Ltd closed the week with a notable 9.44% gain, rising from Rs.18.00 to Rs.19.70, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. Despite the strong price appreciation on the final trading day, the week was marked by a downgrade to a ‘Strong Sell’ rating and a nuanced shift in valuation from very attractive to attractive, reflecting mixed financial signals and subdued fundamentals. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Mangalore Refinery & Petrochemicals Ltd Gains 4.93%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: Mangalore Refinery & Petrochemicals Ltd. (MRPL) closed the week ending 6 March 2026 with a notable gain of 4.93%, rising from Rs.196.65 to Rs.206.35. This performance stands in stark contrast to the Sensex, which declined 3.00% over the same period, underscoring MRPL’s relative strength amid a volatile market backdrop. The week was marked by a sharp opening gap down, followed by a strong recovery and a surge to new 52-week highs, supported by robust volume and an upgraded Mojo Grade. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: JTL Industries Drops 16.12%: 6 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: JTL Industries Ltd endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price plummeting 16.12% from Rs.63.70 to Rs.53.43, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% decline over the same period. The week was marked by steep gap downs, a fresh 52-week low, and persistent selling pressure amid subdued financial results and cautious market sentiment. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Steelcast Ltd Falls 3.12%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Steelcast Ltd closed the week ending 6 March 2026 at Rs.226.60, down 3.12% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.233.90. This decline slightly outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period, reflecting a challenging week marked by significant volatility, rating downgrades, and technical shifts. Despite some intraday recoveries, the stock faced persistent selling pressure amid mixed fundamentals and cautious market sentiment. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Axis Bank Ltd. Falls 4.91%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Axis Bank Ltd. closed the week ending 6 March 2026 down 4.91%, underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% decline. The stock faced sustained selling pressure amid mixed technical signals and heightened derivatives activity, with key events including an intraday low on 4 March, heavy call and put option volumes, and continued high-value trading despite a six-day losing streak. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Moschip Technologies Falls 12.10%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Moschip Technologies Ltd endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its share price declining sharply by 12.10%, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% fall. The stock opened the week with a steep 7.20% gap down on 2 March amid heightened selling pressure and technical weakness, followed by continued volatility and modest recovery attempts that ultimately failed to sustain gains. This review analyses the key events and price movements that shaped Moschip’s difficult week in the market. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Tarmat Ltd Falls 9.17%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Tarmat Ltd’s shares declined sharply by 9.17% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.64.49 from Rs.71.00 the previous Friday. This underperformance contrasted with the broader Sensex’s 3.00% fall, highlighting intensified selling pressure on the micro-cap construction stock amid valuation concerns and technical weakness. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Symphony Ltd Falls 4.82%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Downtrend
item.description: Symphony Ltd’s shares declined by 4.82% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.756.05, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows during the week amid a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO, driven by deteriorating financials, bearish technical indicators, and valuation concerns. Despite some operational strengths, the company’s recent quarterly results and market sentiment weighed heavily on its price performance. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: HMT Ltd Gains 12.15%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: HMT Ltd delivered a remarkable 12.15% gain over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.68.65 from Rs.61.21 the previous Friday. This performance sharply contrasted with the Sensex’s 3.00% decline over the same period, highlighting HMT’s strong relative momentum amid a volatile market backdrop. The week was marked by dramatic price swings, including two upper circuit hits and a lower circuit event, reflecting intense investor interest and shifting sentiment in the industrial manufacturing stock. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Brigade Enterprises Ltd Falls 3.78%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Brigade Enterprises Ltd’s stock declined by 3.78% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.665.95 from Rs.692.10 the previous Friday. The stock underperformed the Sensex, which fell 3.00% during the same period. The week was marked by fresh 52-week lows, valuation adjustments, and mixed market sentiment amid sectoral pressures in real estate. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Nagreeka Capital & Infrastructure Ltd Drops 0.38% Amid Sharp Volatility: 3 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Nagreeka Capital & Infrastructure Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 2 to 6 March 2026, closing marginally lower by 0.38% at Rs.25.91 despite a sharp 3.00% decline in the Sensex. The stock’s price action was marked by extreme volatility, including a plunge to a 52-week low and a lower circuit lock on 2 March, followed by a partial recovery midweek. Overall, Nagreeka outperformed the broader market but remained under pressure amid persistent selling and weak technical indicators. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: GMR Airports Declines 5.61%: Mixed Signals Amid Volatility and Downgrade
item.description: GMR Airports Ltd experienced a challenging week on the bourses, with its share price declining by 5.61% from Rs.100.65 to Rs.95.00, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The stock faced significant volatility, including a sharp gap down and intraday lows early in the week, followed by mixed technical signals and a downgrade in analyst sentiment. Despite some recovery midweek, the overall trend remained bearish amid sectoral pressures and cautious market sentiment. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Panama Petrochem Ltd Falls 2.10%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Panama Petrochem Ltd’s shares declined by 2.10% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.275.50 compared to Rs.281.40 the previous Friday. This underperformance came despite the broader Sensex falling 3.00% over the same period, indicating a relative resilience amid persistent bearish momentum and sectoral headwinds. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Garware Hi Tech Films Ltd Falls 3.63%: Downgrade and Technical Shifts Shape the Week
item.description: Garware Hi Tech Films Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.4,051.65 on 6 March 2026, down 3.63% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.4,204.15. This decline slightly outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% drop over the same period, reflecting a combination of technical deterioration, a significant rating downgrade, and mixed market sentiment. Despite some short-term rebounds late in the week, the stock’s overall momentum was weighed down by bearish signals and valuation concerns. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Chennai Petroleum Gains 4.57%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Momentum
item.description: Chennai Petroleum Corporation Ltd (CPCL) delivered a resilient performance this week, closing with a 4.57% gain to Rs.1,007.00 despite a broadly negative market backdrop where the Sensex declined 3.00%. The stock’s recovery from an early-week sharp decline and robust trading activity midweek underpinned its relative outperformance, highlighting renewed investor interest amid strong technical and institutional signals. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: GHV Infra Projects Ltd Falls 8.86%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: GHV Infra Projects Ltd experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its share price declining by 8.86% to close at Rs.313.80, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant gap down openings on 2 and 4 March, reflecting market caution amid mixed technical signals and sector-specific pressures. Despite the short-term weakness, the stock remains above key longer-term moving averages, indicating underlying medium- to long-term strength. Time: 1 hour ago
